exampleSentences: Em cần một khuôn bánh để làm bánh kem này.
Anh đã mua thêm khuôn cho dự án thủ công của mình.
Chị có thể cho em mượn khuôn để làm xà phòng không?
notes:
Khuôn usually means a mold — a shaped container you pour something into so it takes that shape. It’s a countable noun, often used with cái (cái khuôn), and pairs a lot with things you shape: khuôn bánh, khuôn đúc.

It also means a fixed form or pattern, especially in khuôn mẫu (a model/standard to follow).  

Very common: khuôn mặt = face (literally “face shape”), so don’t always think about baking.  

Think of it as “a shape that makes other things take shape,” both physically and socially.

translation.en: I need a mold to make cake.

content.sentence: Tôi cần một ___ để làm bánh.

example.en: She used a mold to make the cake.
example.vi: Cô ấy đã sử dụng một khuôn để làm bánh.
example.es: Ella usó un molde para hacer el pastel.

definitions.es: Un recipiente hueco utilizado para dar forma a un material líquido o caliente cuando se enfría y se endurece.
definitions.vi: Một cái hộp rỗng được sử dụng để tạo hình cho vật liệu lỏng hoặc nóng khi nó nguội và cứng lại.
definitions.en: A hollow container used to give shape to molten or hot liquid material when it cools and hardens.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I need a mold to make this cake.
I bought more mold for my craft project.
Can you lend me a mold to make soap?
notes_md:
**Khuôn** usually means a *mold* — a shaped container you pour something into so it takes that shape. It’s a countable noun, often used with **cái** (cái khuôn), and pairs a lot with things you shape: khuôn bánh, khuôn đúc.

It also means a fixed *form or pattern*, especially in **khuôn mẫu** (a model/standard to follow).  

Very common: **khuôn mặt** = *face* (literally “face shape”), so don’t always think about baking.  

Think of it as “a shape that makes other things take shape,” both physically and socially.

content.sentence: Họ bán ___ cho các đầu bếp.
